decisions.title: Skill Structure - Progressive Disclosure
decisions.original_question: How should the skill content be organized for different expertise levels?
decisions.decision: Use progressive disclosure: SKILL.md covers the 80% use case, reference files provide depth for advanced scenarios.
decisions.implication: Engineers should be productive from SKILL.md alone. Reference files must be self-contained.

decisions.title: Target Audience - Intermediate Engineers
decisions.original_question: What level of expertise should the skill assume?
decisions.decision: Assume intermediate expertise with focus on patterns and trade-offs.
decisions.implication: Avoid basic syntax explanations; emphasize architecture, performance, and edge cases.

decisions.title: Code Examples - Copy Paste Ready
decisions.original_question: What format should code examples use?
decisions.decision: Include production-ready code templates with error handling and logging.
decisions.implication: No pseudocode; examples should run with minimal adaptation.

decisions.title: Architecture - Decision Matrix
decisions.original_question: How should architectural choices be presented?
decisions.decision: Use decision matrices with clear criteria and explicit pattern recommendations.
decisions.implication: Each matrix cell needs concrete thresholds and a recommendation, not generic advice.

decisions.title: Testing Strategy Tension
decisions.original_question: What testing approach should the skill recommend?
decisions.decision: Coverage and delivery-speed goals conflict and require user review.
decisions.implication: Needs explicit prioritization between strict coverage gates and delivery velocity.

decisions.title: Error Handling - Recovery Paths
decisions.original_question: How should error handling be structured?
decisions.decision: Use explicit error categories with detection, impact, recovery, and prevention.
decisions.implication: Every documented failure mode must include a concrete recovery path.

decisions.title: Performance - Threshold Driven
decisions.original_question: How should performance guidance be structured?
decisions.decision: Define measurable thresholds and an optimization sequence.
decisions.implication: Thresholds should be adjustable and tied to specific measurement tooling.

decisions.title: Security - Defense in Depth
decisions.original_question: What security approach should the skill recommend?
decisions.decision: Layer controls across validation, auth, secrets, and dependency scanning.
decisions.implication: Security layers must be independently testable and reviewed via checklist.

decisions.title: Monitoring Stack Ambiguity
decisions.original_question: What monitoring approach should be recommended?
decisions.decision: Monitoring tooling is inconsistent and needs user confirmation.
decisions.implication: Cannot finalize tool-specific recommendations until stack constraints are confirmed.

decisions.title: Documentation - ADRs
decisions.original_question: How should design decisions be documented in the output?
decisions.decision: Use ADRs for significant design decisions.
decisions.implication: ADRs should be stored with code and map back to decisions captured here.

decisions.title: Deployment - Blue Green
decisions.original_question: What deployment strategy should be recommended?
decisions.decision: Use blue-green deployments with health checks and automated rollback thresholds.
decisions.implication: This resolves conflicting asks for rolling deploys plus near-instant rollback.

decisions.title: Configuration - Environment Aware
decisions.decision: Use environment variables for sensitive and environment-specific values.
decisions.implication: Validate configuration at startup and fail fast on missing required values.
